# Vocabulary Projection — From Vectors to Words {#sec-ch11-vocab-projection}

After `N` transformer blocks, we have a matrix $X_{\text{final}} \in \mathbb{R}^{T\times d}$.
The final row, `X_final[T-1]`, is a rich contextualized representation of the last token.
The last step is to project this vector into a probability distribution over the vocabulary: which token is most likely to come next?

This projection is called the *language model head*, or *unembedding layer*.

{{< include book/11-vocab-projection/sections/idea.qmd >}}

{{< include book/11-vocab-projection/sections/math.qmd >}}

{{< include book/11-vocab-projection/sections/generation.qmd >}}

{{< include book/11-vocab-projection/sections/matrix.qmd >}}

{{< include book/11-vocab-projection/sections/code.qmd >}}

{{< include book/11-vocab-projection/sections/takeaways.qmd >}}
